reviews.text:
This is definitely a case of looking better in a picture than what it actually is.

The metal is very thin and when I placed some faux flowers in it, the vase had a hard time standing on its own. Looks like a filler place kindve vase and even then, it’s not all that attractive in person. There is definitely better made to be had.

I wouldn’t recommend this vase.

reviews.title: GORGEOUS UNIQUE VASE
reviews.text:
This vase is absolutely gorgeous - while it looks interesting in this listing, it is so much better in person. This is the type of thing where people will stop and ask you where you found it. I have seen similar items selling for many times this amount in art galleries and admired them - but I love owning and being able to actually use this one!

The combination of wood and the swirled cobalt colored glass is exquisite. Should you wish the wood could be painted with chalk paint or lightly sanded and painted with a latex or oil based paint. Thus far I have left mine the way it came but may change it in the future on a whim. There really is no need as it looks great - I just enjoy doing that type of thing.

This vase can host a variety of objects for different looks. I have used it for displaying fresh flowers with clear water beads, half filled with river rocks with an electric votive and as part of a table centerpiece with dried flowers. Each time I had multiple people comment on just how unique and beautiful it is.

This is definitely a product I would recommend whether you are looking for a unique vase or a gift for someone you care about.

The modern Omaggio vase was inspired by Tulle Emborg, one of the talented women painters in Kahler's Danish workshop in the 1930s and 1940s. As in the old Kahler workshop, each and every stripe has been hand-painted by talented women painters. The vases then go through multiple hand processes in connection with glazing, quality checking and fine polishing. The design has been reinterpreted and revived by the talented designer duo Stilleben (Ditte Reckweg and Jelena Schou Nordentoft). "We have always been totally fascinated by Kahler's epoch-making ceramics, which have played an enormous part in putting Denmark on the map when it comes to art and design. It is a great pleasure to help bring the art ceramics heritage into a new era and a new form language", say the two designers. It is Kahler's mission to create accessible art for the people. Now a leading Scandinavian design brand, their roots go back to 1839 as a small Danish pottery producer. For more than 175 years, their tradition has been to collaborate with significant artists to provide distinctive and contemporary collections. Kahler is firmly established as part of Danish cultural heritage, and they strive to preserve history and traditions in each handmade design. Height 80mm (3.1 In.) Diameter 50mm (2 In.)
